source_test: bespoke (Phase 4 Task 2) -- ComputeDamages/AggregateResults were severed from the Phase 3 Inventory port pending HEC.FDA.Model.metrics.ConsequenceResult (Phase 4 Task 1), so there is no direct upstream unit test to reproduce; this fixture is designed from Inventory.ComputeDamages/AggregateResults's own source to lock the faithful other/vehicle store-swap documented in inventory.hpp's compute_damages doc comment.
note: construct is {occ_types: [<occupancy_type construct, same shape as structures/occupancy_type.json's 'construct', plus OPTIONAL vehicle_damages/other_damages (paired-data yvals over the shared 'depths' array) and vehicle_value/other_value (ValueUncertainty construct, same shape as structure_value)>, ...], structures: [<structure construct, same shape as structures/structure.json's 'construct.structure', with val_vehic/val_other set>, ...], wses: [[<per-structure WSE for profile 0>, ...], ...] ([profile][structure], a value of -9999 skips that structure/profile per the faithful C# 'wse != -9999' guard), analysis_year, damage_category, sample: [iteration, computeIsDeterministic], [price_index]}. The single case below uses one occupancy type ('MyOccupancyType') shared by all 3 structures, with DISTINCT struct/content/vehicle/other depth-percent-damage curves (percentages 10x/10x/4x/nonlinear-per-depth respectively) and distinct val_vehic/val_other per structure, so that a wiring mistake in compute_damages/aggregate_results's other/vehicle argument order would change the emitted vehicle_damage/other_damage arrays (unlike an all-zero or identical-curve fixture, which the swap-then-swap-back identity would pass trivially either way -- see inventory.hpp for the algebraic proof that the two swaps compose to the identity in CORRECTLY-wired code). Profile 1's structure '2' uses wse=-9999 to also exercise the skip guard (contributes 0 to every profile-1 total). Sample uses iteration=1, computeIsDeterministic=true (matching structures/occupancy_type.json's 'sample_deterministic' case), so struct/content/vehicle/other percent-damage curves and all four value-uncertainty offsets resolve to their curve/distribution means deterministically -- avoiding any RNG-seed dependency in this fixture. csvr (central=90) drives content damage (sampled_structure_value * csvr/100), so val_cont is unset (unused). All four `compute_damages_struct/_content/_vehicle/_other` expected arrays are gate-pinned ('PIN' until captured against the real C#), one value per wses profile.
